edges.description: ARSA pathogenic variants reduce arylsulfatase A enzyme activity.
Loss of arylsulfatase A activity leads directly to pathologic sulfatide storage.
Stored sulfatides are the core chemical biomarker of the lysosomal storage lesion.
Excess sulfatides are excreted in urine and provide a diagnostic biochemical readout.
Sulfatide accumulation contributes directly to demyelination within the CNS.
Sulfatide storage also contributes to demyelinating peripheral nerve disease.
Sulfatide storage initiates the MLD disease cascade that includes broader neurodegeneration of the central and peripheral nervous systems.
Progressive CNS demyelination contributes to worsening cognitive dysfunction.
Early-onset demyelinating disease leads to loss of acquired motor abilities in childhood forms.
Peripheral nerve demyelination produces the polyneuropathy phenotype seen in MLD.
Progressive neurologic deterioration leads to bulbar dysfunction and dysphagia.
Ongoing neurodegeneration contributes to seizures in early-onset MLD.
Progressive late-infantile neurologic deterioration includes hypotonia.
Reduced ARSA enzyme activity reports the proximal enzyme deficiency that initiates MLD.
Increased sulfatide burden reports the core lysosomal storage lesion in MLD.
Increased urinary sulfatide excretion is a diagnostic readout of the sulfatide storage mechanism.

evidence.snippet: Metachromatic leukodystrophy (MLD) is a rare autosomal recessive lysosomal storage disorder caused by disease-causing variants in the gene coding for arylsulfatase A, leading to deficient enzyme activity and subsequent accumulation of sulfatides.
ARSA | HGNC:713 | metachromatic leukodystrophy | MONDO:0018868 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Disease-causing ARSA variants reduce arylsulfatase A activity in MLD.

evidence.snippet: Metachromatic leukodystrophy (MLD) is a rare autosomal recessive lysosomal storage disorder caused by disease-causing variants in the gene coding for arylsulfatase A, leading to deficient enzyme activity and subsequent accumulation of sulfatides.

nodes.description: Autologous hematopoietic stem cell gene therapy using arsa-cel preserves motor and cognitive development in selected early-onset patients.

evidence.snippet: Treatment with arsa-cel resulted in sustained, clinically relevant benefits in children with early-onset MLD by preserving cognitive function and motor development in most patients, and slowing demyelination and brain atrophy.

Research advancements have enabled improved understanding of the effects of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation and the development of novel therapeutic approaches, including hematopoietic stem cell gene therapy, which is now authorized in the EU, United Kingdom, and United States as treatment for selected patients with early-onset forms of MLD.

nodes.description: MLD causes progressive demyelination within the central nervous system, driving cognitive and developmental decline.

evidence.snippet: MLD is characterized by demyelination and neurodegeneration of the central and peripheral nervous system, manifesting as progressive motor and cognitive defects in affected individuals.

nodes.description: Progressive cognitive decline is a major clinical manifestation of MLD.

evidence.snippet: MLD is characterized by demyelination and neurodegeneration of the central and peripheral nervous system, manifesting as progressive motor and cognitive defects in affected individuals.

nodes.description: MLD also causes demyelinating disease in the peripheral nervous system, producing a clinically important polyneuropathy burden.

evidence.snippet: This case series, however, illustrates the occurrence of severely progressive polyneuropathy shortly after HCT in two patients with late-infantile, one with late-juvenile, and one with adult MLD, leading to the inability to walk or sit without support.

nodes.description: Motor skill regression is typical of early-onset MLD.

evidence.snippet: The typical clinical course of patients with the late infantile form includes a regression in motor skills with progression to dysphagia, seizures, hypotonia and death.

nodes.description: Bulbar dysfunction with dysphagia develops as late-infantile MLD progresses.

evidence.snippet: The typical clinical course of patients with the late infantile form includes a regression in motor skills with progression to dysphagia, seizures, hypotonia and death.

nodes.description: Allogeneic HSCT is most useful before disease onset or in very early disease stages.

evidence.snippet: Both hematopoietic stem cell transplantation and hematopoietic stem cell gene therapy are most effective when administered before disease onset.

nodes.description: Hypotonia develops during progression of late-infantile MLD.

evidence.snippet: The typical clinical course of patients with the late infantile form includes a regression in motor skills with progression to dysphagia, seizures, hypotonia and death.

nodes.description: Progressive demyelinating polyneuropathy contributes substantially to disability in MLD.

evidence.snippet: This case series, however, illustrates the occurrence of severely progressive polyneuropathy shortly after HCT in two patients with late-infantile, one with late-juvenile, and one with adult MLD, leading to the inability to walk or sit without support.

nodes.description: Alongside demyelination, MLD includes a broader neurodegenerative process that contributes to progressive neurologic disability.

evidence.snippet: MLD is characterized by demyelination and neurodegeneration of the central and peripheral nervous system, manifesting as progressive motor and cognitive defects in affected individuals.
Measurement of arylsulfatase A in that laboratory showed a partial decrease in arylsulfatase A activity measured under typical conditions

readouts.interpretation: Reduced ARSA enzyme activity reports the proximal enzyme deficiency that initiates MLD.

nodes.description: Seizures occur in the progressive late-infantile disease course.

evidence.snippet: The typical clinical course of patients with the late infantile form includes a regression in motor skills with progression to dysphagia, seizures, hypotonia and death.

nodes.description: Pathologic sulfatide storage is the immediate biochemical consequence of arylsulfatase A deficiency in MLD.

evidence.snippet: Metachromatic leukodystrophy (MLD) is a rare autosomal recessive lysosomal storage disorder caused by disease-causing variants in the gene coding for arylsulfatase A, leading to deficient enzyme activity and subsequent accumulation of sulfatides.

Metachromatic leukodystrophy (MLD) is a rare autosomal recessive lysosomal storage disorder caused by disease-causing variants in the gene coding for arylsulfatase A, leading to deficient enzyme activity and subsequent accumulation of sulfatides.

readouts.interpretation: Increased sulfatide burden reports the core lysosomal storage lesion in MLD.

evidence.snippet: The excretion of sulfatides in the urine is a definitive test to confirm the diagnosis of MLD, whether caused by defects in ARSA or saposin B.

readouts.interpretation: Increased urinary sulfatide excretion is a diagnostic readout of the sulfatide storage mechanism.
